user_query: I’m Uncle Ray, a jazz musician, and I want to write a heartfelt blog post to my nephew about how I first introduced him to music. Please: 1) Read and use config/site_config.yaml (don’t hardcode values) to determine the blog’s naming, tone, and where to save outputs. 2) Use a general-purpose web search engine to find at least the number of sources specified by min_sources in the config (from reputable sources such as cultural institutions, universities, or official artist organizations). Research two items: (a) brief historical/context notes about the song listed under first_introduced_song and (b) a concise, plain-English explainer of one foundational jazz concept (e.g., swing feel or blue notes). Paraphrase; do not copy long passages. 3) Save a structured research log to {research_dir from config}/sources.json (create directories if needed). Each entry must be a JSON object with fields: query (the exact search query string you used), source_title, source_domain, url, notes (1–2 sentences, paraphrased), used_for ("song_history" or "concept_explainer"). 4) Write the blog post to {drafts_dir from config}/a-letter-to-{nephew_name-from-config}-about-the-music.md in Markdown with YAML front matter containing: title ("{site_name}: A Letter to {nephew_name}"), author (author.display_name), category (front_matter.category), tags (all default_tags from config plus "letter"), and fact_check_source_count (the number of entries in sources.json). The body must include these sections and content: - "## How it started": a warm, conversational recollection of introducing {first_introduced_song} on {first_instrument}. - "## Listening guide": three bullet-point recommendations (1) a performance/recording of the introduced song to revisit, (2) one classic album, (3) one modern artist to explore; include a 1–2 sentence blurb for each (draw on your research where relevant). - "## Sources consulted": list each source_domain and source_title found in sources.json as bullet points. Ensure the tone follows tone_guide from the config, avoid jargon unless briefly explained, and do not place direct URLs in the post body (URLs should only appear in sources.json). Inputs: config/site_config.yaml. Outputs: (a) {research_dir}/sources.json and (b) {drafts_dir}/a-letter-to-{nephew_name}-about-the-music.md. The value of fact_check_source_count in the post’s front matter must equal the number of entries in sources.json, and tags must include all default_tags from the config. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
